title: Branchwater (bw) v0 — git for your local databases
overview: Branchwater (bw) is an engine-agnostic TypeScript/Node CLI that snapshots all of a developer's configured local databases as one logical unit (a branch), lets them experiment freely, and rolls back in seconds. v0 ships the full architectural spine (orchestrator + adapter contract + JSON manifest) plus exactly one adapter (Postgres via pg_dump/pg_restore) and six CLI commands. The hard requirement is engine-agnosticism: adding MySQL/Redis/ZFS later must mean 'write a new adapter' with zero changes to the core.
scopeChallenge: Greenfield repo (only .claude/ exists), so EXPANSION governs decomposition granularity and test depth, NOT feature scope. Phase 0 decisions confirmed with user: git-faithful branch model (immutable snapshots + movable branch pointers + HEAD); JSON manifest behind a repository interface (SQLite swappable later); auto safety-snapshot before destructive checkout; atomic manifest writes (temp+rename). Ruled out of v0: a second adapter, solving cross-engine write consistency (noted not solved), remote storage, PITR, multi-process locking. The ZFS/btrfs copy-on-write backend is designed-for (the adapter contract accommodates an opaque snapshot id and orchestrator-owned storageDir) but not built. Overriding risk: any Postgres specific concept leaking above the adapter boundary into core/manifest/CLI — guarded by eslint no-restricted-imports (TASK-002) and a runtime boundary test (TASK-024).

failureModes.risk: Postgres specifics leak above the adapter boundary into core/CLI
failureModes.mitigation: eslint no-restricted-imports (TASK-002) + runtime arch test (TASK-024) + orchestrator tested only with FakeAdapter (TASK-020)

failureModes.risk: Multi-engine write consistency (engines snapshotted at slightly different moments)
failureModes.mitigation: v0 = sequential best-effort, documented caveat (TASK-025); manifest written only after all engines succeed; future --quiesce hook noted

failureModes.risk: Destructive checkout silently discards current state
failureModes.mitigation: Auto safety-snapshot before restore; surface autosave id; confirmation prompt unless --yes

failureModes.risk: Partial multi-engine snapshot leaves orphan artifacts
failureModes.mitigation: Rollback: delete partial engine artifacts and abort before writing the manifest

failureModes.risk: Partial multi-engine restore leaves engines split across branches (checkout half-applied)
failureModes.mitigation: Auto safety-snapshot taken first; on mid-checkout failure, report restored-vs-failed engines and point to the autosave for recovery; documented as best-effort (no cross-engine restore transaction in v0)
Atomic write (temp file + rename); schema-validate on load

failureModes.risk: pg_restore blocked by live connections / ownership errors
Shell injection via connection strings / names
failureModes.mitigation: spawn with argv array, never a shell string

failureModes.risk: Snapshot GC deletes a snapshot still shared by another branch
failureModes.mitigation: Reference-count snapshots across all branches before GC; unit-tested (TASK-018)

failureModes.risk: CI fails without a live Postgres
failureModes.mitigation: Integration test skips cleanly when no DB; gated behind env flag

testCoverageMap.codepath: Config load + env interpolation + validation failures
Postgres adapter argv construction + artifact paths
Full six-command CLI happy path + error exits
No adapter import in core/CLI commands

tasks.title: Project scaffold — package, TS config, gitignore
tasks.description: Create package.json (bin.bw -> dist/cli/index.js, scripts: build/typecheck/lint/format/test, deps: commander/picocolors/spinner/zod, devDeps: typescript/jest/ts-jest/@types/eslint/prettier/tsx), strict tsconfig.json (strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, ES2022, outDir dist), and .gitignore (node_modules, dist, .bw). Create the src/ skeleton.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install succeeds and npx tsc --noEmit runs with zero config errors on an empty src/
package.json declares bin.bw pointing at the built CLI entry
tsconfig has strict: true and noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true (a non-strict file would error)

tasks.title: Lint, format & test tooling + engine-boundary lint rule
tasks.description: Create eslint.config.js, .prettierrc, jest.config.js (ts-jest, testMatch test/**/*.test.ts). Add eslint no-restricted-imports forbidding any file under src/core/** or src/cli/** from importing src/adapters/** — the static half of the engine-agnostic guard.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run lint and npm run format -- --check execute cleanly on the skeleton
npm test runs jest (zero tests pass without error)
A deliberate import from src/core/foo.ts to src/adapters/postgres triggers an eslint error

tasks.title: Adapter contract — EngineAdapter interface & value types
tasks.description: Create src/core/adapter/types.ts defining EngineSnapshotId, AdapterContext, AdapterLogger, SnapshotResult, EngineSnapshotInfo, EngineAdapter, AdapterFactory. Pure types, no engine imports. config is typed unknown; opaque EngineSnapshotId; orchestrator-owned storageDir. Document the agnosticism rationale per member.
acceptanceCriteria: All seven exports compile under strict mode with no runtime code
AdapterContext.config is typed unknown (no engine-specific fields anywhere)
File imports nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: Manifest schema & types
tasks.description: Create src/core/manifest/types.ts (Manifest, BranchRef, SnapshotRecord) and src/core/manifest/schema.ts (zod manifestSchema mirroring the types). Reject unknown version values and malformed records.
acceptanceCriteria: manifestSchema.parse() accepts a valid manifest with branches + snapshots + HEAD
Parsing version: 2 or a branch pointing at a missing snapshot id is rejected
Types and zod schema stay structurally in sync

tasks.title: Config schema & types
tasks.description: Create src/core/config/types.ts (BwConfig, EngineConfigEntry) and src/core/config/schema.ts (zod bwConfigSchema). connection is z.record(z.unknown()) — deliberately opaque so core stays engine-agnostic. Enforce unique engine names.
acceptanceCriteria: bwConfigSchema.parse() accepts a config with one or more engines
Duplicate engine names are rejected with a clear error
connection accepts arbitrary keys (no Postgres fields hardcoded in core)

tasks.title: Core utilities — safe exec & id generation
tasks.description: Create src/util/exec.ts (Promise wrapper over child_process.spawn — argv array, NO shell interpolation; captures stdout/stderr; honors AbortSignal; rejects with exit code + stderr) and src/util/ids.ts (collision-resistant id generator, e.g. snap_<ulid>).
acceptanceCriteria: exec() resolves with stdout for zero-exit and rejects with stderr for non-zero
Arguments with shell metacharacters are passed literally, not interpreted
newId() returns unique values across 10k calls

tasks.title: Terminal output — logger & spinner
tasks.description: Create src/util/logger.ts (picocolors logger implementing AdapterLogger from TASK-003: info/warn/error/success/debug; respects verbose/quiet and a --json mode that suppresses decoration) and src/util/spinner.ts (start/succeed/fail/stop wrapper).
acceptanceCriteria: Logger satisfies AdapterLogger (compile-checked assignability)
In --json mode no spinner/color escape codes are written to stdout
debug() output is suppressed unless verbose is enabled

tasks.title: Adapter registry
tasks.description: Create src/core/adapter/registry.ts: register AdapterFactory by type string and resolve an EngineAdapter for a config entry. Throws a clear error for unknown types. Depends only on the TASK-003 contract — never a concrete adapter.
acceptanceCriteria: register('postgres', factory) then resolve('postgres') returns a fresh adapter instance
resolve('redis') (unregistered) throws naming the missing type and listing known types
registry.ts imports nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: Config loader with env-var interpolation
tasks.description: Create src/core/config/load.ts: locate + read bw.config.json (honor --config/--cwd), interpolate ${ENV_VAR} in string values from process.env, then validate with bwConfigSchema. Throw actionable errors for missing file, bad JSON, validation failure, or unresolved env var.
acceptanceCriteria: Loads + validates a well-formed config and resolves ${PGPASSWORD} from env
Missing config file produces a clear 'run bw init' style error (not a raw ENOENT)
An unresolved ${MISSING} env reference fails loudly rather than substituting empty string

tasks.title: Manifest store (JSON repository, atomic write + GC)
tasks.description: Create src/core/manifest/store.ts: a ManifestStore class backing .bw/manifest.json behind a repository interface (SQLite swappable later). Methods: init(), load() (schema-validated), save() (atomic temp+rename), createSnapshot(), getBranch/setBranch/deleteBranch(), setHead(), gcUnreferencedSnapshots().
acceptanceCriteria: init() then load() round-trips; save() never leaves a partial file if interrupted (temp+rename)
gcUnreferencedSnapshots() returns exactly the snapshots no branch references and keeps shared ones
load() on a corrupt/invalid manifest.json throws via schema validation, not a silent default

tasks.title: Postgres pg_dump/pg_restore command wrappers
tasks.description: Create src/adapters/postgres/pgtools.ts: build/run pg_dump -Fc to a target file, pg_restore --clean --if-exists --no-owner from a file, plus a helper to terminate competing connections (pg_terminate_backend via psql). Use the TASK-006 exec() wrapper; surface tool-not-installed and auth errors clearly.
acceptanceCriteria: Builds a correct pg_dump -Fc argv targeting a given file + connection
Builds a pg_restore --clean --if-exists --no-owner argv for that file
Missing pg_dump binary on PATH produces a clear 'install postgresql-client' error

tasks.title: PostgresAdapter implementing EngineAdapter
tasks.description: Create src/adapters/postgres/index.ts (PostgresAdapter implements EngineAdapter, type='postgres') and src/adapters/postgres/config.ts (zod schema for the connection block: url OR host/port/user/password/database). snapshot() dumps to <storageDir>/<id>.dump and returns { id }; restore() runs pg_restore; list()/delete() manage .dump files; validate() checks reachability. All PG specifics live here.
acceptanceCriteria: snapshot() returns an opaque id and writes a dump file under the provided storageDir
restore() of a non-existent id throws rather than silently no-op'ing
The connection zod schema rejects a block missing both url and host+database

tasks.title: Orchestrator (the brain)
tasks.description: Create src/core/orchestrator.ts: a class constructed with registry, config, manifest store, and logger (via TASK-003/008/009/010 interfaces — never imports a concrete adapter). Implements snapshot(msg?) (call every engine's snapshot(), bundle ids into one SnapshotRecord, advance HEAD; on any engine failure delete partial artifacts + abort before writing manifest), branch(name), checkout(name) (auto safety-snapshot -> restore all engines -> move HEAD), list(), delete(name) (+ GC artifacts via adapters).
acceptanceCriteria: snapshot() over 2 fake engines produces one SnapshotRecord whose engines map has both ids and advances HEAD
If one engine's snapshot() throws, no manifest entry is written and the other engine's partial artifact is deleted
checkout() creates an autosave snapshot of current state before calling restore() on each engine
If one engine's restore() fails mid-checkout, the orchestrator reports which engines were restored vs failed and surfaces the autosave snapshot id for recovery (no silent split-brain)
orchestrator.ts imports nothing from src/adapters/**

tasks.title: CLI commands — init & snapshot
tasks.description: Create src/cli/commands/init.ts (scaffold bw.config.json if absent, create .bw/ via ManifestStore.init(), capture a root snapshot on branch main) and src/cli/commands/snapshot.ts (parse -m/--message, call orchestrator.snapshot(), print result). Commands receive a constructed orchestrator; no business logic in the command layer.
acceptanceCriteria: init is idempotent-safe: re-running on an initialized repo warns instead of clobbering the manifest
snapshot -m 'msg' records the message on the new SnapshotRecord
snapshot before init exits non-zero with a clear 'run bw init first' message

tasks.title: CLI commands — branch & checkout
tasks.description: Create src/cli/commands/branch.ts (orchestrator.branch(name)) and src/cli/commands/checkout.ts (orchestrator.checkout(name); confirm unless --yes since checkout is destructive; surface the autosave snapshot name so the user knows how to undo).
acceptanceCriteria: branch <name> creates a pointer at the current snapshot without switching HEAD
checkout <name> prints the autosave snapshot id created before restore
checkout <missing-branch> exits non-zero without touching any database

tasks.title: CLI commands — list & delete
tasks.description: Create src/cli/commands/list.ts (render branches with * for current, snapshots, lineage; support --json) and src/cli/commands/delete.ts (orchestrator.delete(name); confirm unless --yes; report GC'd snapshots/artifacts).
acceptanceCriteria: list marks the current branch and renders machine-readable output under --json
delete <name> removes the branch and GCs only snapshots no other branch references
delete of the current/HEAD branch is refused with a clear error

tasks.title: CLI entry, global flags & adapter registration
tasks.description: Create src/cli/index.ts: the commander program with global flags (--config, --cwd, --json, --yes, --verbose), shebang, centralized error handling (clean messages + non-zero exit codes; full stack only under --verbose), and a bootstrap that registers PostgresAdapter into the registry (the one place core meets the concrete adapter — the composition root). Wire in all six commands.
acceptanceCriteria: bw --help lists all six commands and global flags
An uncaught orchestrator error exits non-zero with a single clean line (full stack only under --verbose)
Postgres is the only adapter registered, and registration happens in the entry (not in core)

tasks.title: Unit tests — manifest store
tasks.description: Create test/manifest/store.test.ts: cover round-trip save/load, atomic-write behavior, branch pointer ops, HEAD updates, and GC of unreferenced vs shared snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests pass for create -> save -> load round-trip and for GC keeping shared snapshots
A test asserts load() throws on a corrupted manifest file
A test asserts no partial file remains if save() is interrupted before rename

tasks.title: Unit tests — adapter registry & config loader
tasks.description: Create test/core/registry.test.ts and test/config/load.test.ts: cover register/resolve, unknown-type error, env interpolation, and validation failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolving an unregistered type throws with the known-types list
Config loader resolves ${VAR} and fails on an unresolved var
Invalid config (duplicate engine names) is rejected by the loader test

tasks.title: Unit tests — orchestrator with a FAKE in-memory adapter
tasks.description: Create test/core/orchestrator.test.ts using a FakeAdapter (in-memory, implements EngineAdapter). Primary proof of engine-agnosticism: the orchestrator must work end-to-end with a non-Postgres adapter. Cover multi-engine snapshot bundling, partial-failure rollback, checkout auto-safety-snapshot, and delete+GC.
acceptanceCriteria: Full snapshot -> branch -> checkout -> delete flow passes using ONLY the FakeAdapter (no Postgres)
A simulated mid-snapshot engine failure leaves the manifest unchanged and cleans partial artifacts
Checkout is shown to create an autosave snapshot before any restore() call

tasks.title: Unit tests — Postgres adapter (mocked exec)
tasks.description: Create test/adapters/postgres.test.ts: mock the TASK-006 exec/pgtools layer and assert the adapter builds correct argv, returns opaque ids, writes/reads/deletes the right artifact paths, and validates the connection schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Asserts snapshot() invokes pg_dump -Fc with the configured connection + storage path
Asserts restore() invokes pg_restore --clean --if-exists
Connection schema test rejects a block with neither url nor host+database

tasks.title: Integration test — Postgres against a real database (gated)
tasks.description: Create test/integration/postgres.int.test.ts: snapshot -> mutate -> restore against a real Postgres, gated on an env flag / availability (skips cleanly when no DB, e.g. testcontainers or BW_TEST_PG_URL). Verifies data actually returns to the snapshotted state.
acceptanceCriteria: With a real DB available, a row inserted after snapshot is gone after restore
The test skips (not fails) when no Postgres is configured/available
Artifacts are cleaned up after the test run

tasks.title: End-to-end CLI smoke test
tasks.description: Create test/e2e/cli.test.ts: spawn the built bw binary through init -> snapshot -> branch -> checkout -> list -> delete against a gated real Postgres (reuse the TASK-022 gating, e.g. BW_TEST_PG_URL/testcontainers; skips cleanly when absent), asserting exit codes and output. The happy path must use Postgres, NOT the FakeAdapter, because the built binary registers only Postgres (TASK-017). The error-path assertions fail before touching any engine, so they run unconditionally.
acceptanceCriteria: The full six-command happy path (gated on a real Postgres; skipped when absent) exits 0 and list reflects the expected branches
bw checkout <missing> exits non-zero with a clear message (runs without a DB)
bw snapshot before bw init exits non-zero (runs without a DB)

tasks.title: Architecture boundary test (runtime engine-agnostic guard)
tasks.description: Create test/arch/agnostic.test.ts: statically scan src/core/** and src/cli/commands/** source for any import of src/adapters/**, failing if found. Complements the eslint rule (TASK-002) as a CI-enforced backstop.
acceptanceCriteria: Test passes against the real source tree (no leaks present)
Test fails if a fixture core file importing an adapter is introduced
The scan covers both src/core and src/cli/commands (not the entry composition root)

tasks.title: README — pitch, architecture & quickstart
tasks.description: Create README.md: elevator pitch, the orchestrator/adapter/manifest architecture (with diagram + engine boundary), a quickstart (bw init -> snapshot -> branch -> checkout), and an explicit 'Multi-engine write consistency' caveat (v0 snapshots engines sequentially, best-effort; true cross-engine point-in-time consistency requires quiescing writes — future work).
acceptanceCriteria: README contains the elevator pitch, architecture overview, and a copy-pasteable quickstart
The multi-engine consistency limitation is documented explicitly, not glossed over
Every CLI command from the surface table is documented with an example

tasks.title: CI workflow
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml: on push/PR run install -> lint -> typecheck -> test -> build on a current Node LTS. Keep the gated integration test (TASK-022) opt-in so CI is green without a live database.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow runs lint, typecheck, unit tests, and build as required steps
The gated Postgres integration test does not fail CI when no DB is provisioned
Workflow triggers on both push and pull_request

tasks.title: Adapter-authoring guide & example config
tasks.description: Create docs/ADAPTERS.md (step-by-step: implement EngineAdapter, validate your connection block with zod, register in the entry — proving 'new engine = new adapter, zero core change') and bw.config.example.json.
acceptanceCriteria: The guide walks through implementing all five EngineAdapter methods with a non-PG example
It states the zero-core-change rule and points at the registration site (TASK-017)
bw.config.example.json validates against bwConfigSchema
